frontiers.name: const21_backend_specialization
frontiers.rationale: The leaf contains two multiplications by the fixed constant 21 = 3*b. A backend that compiles those as shift/add constant-arithmetic rather than generic field multipliers should shave a few percent.
frontiers.main_risk: Exact savings depend on the primitive arithmetic library and may already be partly hidden inside the current conservative projection.

frontiers.name: lookup_channel_packing
frontiers.rationale: The current comparison uses explicit 2-channel and 3-channel lookup models. Better qROM/qRAM packing or serial readout could reduce the dominant lookup term.
frontiers.main_risk: This is backend-specific and could trade depth for width or latency in ways the current ISA-level repository does not model.

frontiers.name: cross_call_lazy_reduction
frontiers.rationale: The 28 retained additions are currently modeled as repeated independent leaf calls. A flatter backend could fuse consecutive modular reductions or keep carry-save state live across calls.
frontiers.main_risk: Requires a new exactness layer because it changes the ISA boundary itself.

frontiers.name: tail_restructuring_beyond_2n_over_w_minus_4
frontiers.rationale: Further reducing retained window additions below the public appendix relation would be a real structural gain.
frontiers.main_risk: This may simply be impossible without changing the higher-level algorithm, phase-estimation layout, or error model.

frontiers.name: primitive_gate_cancellation_after_flattening
frontiers.rationale: A real primitive-gate compiler may find cancellations that are invisible at the kickmix ISA level.
frontiers.main_risk: This could also go the other direction if lookup and cleanup abstractions expand badly when flattened.

frontiers.name: signed_digit_recoding
frontiers.rationale: Sparse signed-digit encodings help classically, but coherent reversible recoding has overhead and may erase most of the gain at w=16.
frontiers.main_risk: Easy place for optimistic self-deception; not counted as a likely near-term win here.

stacking_note: Do not multiply the ranges naively. Most of these ideas overlap, and some require a different exactness boundary than the one claimed in this repository.
author_note: This file is a roadmap, not a proved improvement over the audited 880q / 31.0M-32.8M projection.
